lines.en_lit: All the measures an olive-bulk, except for food impure, because the verse changed its expression and the Sages altered the measure The proof of this, Yom Kippur. How did the verse changed its expression? from: shall not be afflicted” How did the Sages change its measure? the volume of a large date-bulk,
And what A proof for this Yom Kippur? If there, I would have said that that is the style of the verse,

From where do we impure foods the volume of an egg-bulk? Rabbi Abbahu said that Rabbi Elazar said that the verse states: “Of all food [okhel] which may be eaten [ye’akhel], food that comes on account of food, And which is that? A chicken egg. And say a kid, It lacks ritual slaughter. And say a ben pekua. requires cutting,
say egg bulk bar yokhani. If you grasped many, you did not grasp anything; if you grasped few, you grasped something. say a very small bird’s egg.
Rabbi Abbahu himself said: “Of all food which may be eaten.” food that you eat at one time. The Sages estimated: The esophagus cannot hold more a chicken’s egg,
Rabbi Elazar said: One who eats fat today must write down the measure lest another court come and increase the measure.

What does increase the measure? If we say will obligate an offering bulk a small olive, - Wasn’t it taught not to be done, and be guilty” one who repents due to his awareness, brings an offering for his unwitting
one who does not repent due to his awareness does not bring an offering for his unwitting
Rather, will not obligate an offering until he has of a large olive,
According to what entered his mind initially, obligate an offering small olive, what increase the measure? perhaps there will be an increase in offerings due to measure

Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Measures and punishments are halakha to Moses from Sinai. The punishments are written Rather, this is said: Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Measures for punishments are halakha to Moses from Sinai.

This was also taught Measures of punishments are halakha to Moses from Sinai. Others say: were instituted by the court of Jabez. Isn’t it written: “These are the mitzvot which the Lord commanded Moses for the children of Israel at Mount Sinai” that a prophet is not permitted to introduce element from here on. Rather, forgot reestablished

one who drinks a cheekful Rabbi Yehuda said Shmuel said: This does not cheeks actually full. Rather, if one pushes to one side and it appears as though his cheek were full. Didn’t we learn A cheekful, Say: Like cheeks full

raises an objection How much drink to be liable? Beit Shammai say: A quarter- and Beit Hillel say: cheeks full. Rabbi Yehuda says in the name of Rabbi Eliezer: Like cheeks full Rabbi Yehuda ben Beteira says: amount swallow
Is preferable to the mishna? Since it was established so that it appears so too, that looks like If so, this is Rabbi Eliezer, between them a paltry cheekful,
Rav Hoshaya strongly objects to this If so, this is Beit Shammai’s leniencies and Beit Hillel’s stringencies, He said to him:
